meta.title: The Inaugural
meta.subtitle: A two-week MedTech research hackathon structured around clinically sourced problem statements and interdisciplinary research teams.
meta.venue: Venue: Bharati Vidyapeeth Medical College, Pune

philosophy.headline: We didn't write the problem statements.
philosophy.subline: We collected them from the field.

timeline.title: Research Abstract Submissions
timeline.description: Open call for medical students across India to submit research abstracts detailing real, unresolved clinical and research bottlenecks. The submission window generated 250+ problem statements from institutions nationwide.

timeline.title: Jury Shortlist Release
timeline.description: An expert jury reviewed all submitted abstracts and shortlisted the Top 5 problem statements based on clinical significance, research depth, and technical tractability. These were released as the official problem statements for Cohort 1.

timeline.title: Cohort 1 Begins — Round 1 Opens
timeline.description: Teams registered and began working on their assigned problem statements. Round 1 ran from 21 to 27 March 2026, with teams developing initial research frameworks and solution architectures.

timeline.title: Round 2 — Finalist Development Phase
timeline.description: Shortlisted teams advanced to Round 2. Daily mentorship sessions were conducted by researchers from the Koita Centre for Digital Health, IIT Bombay through 3 April 2026, providing domain-specific guidance to finalist teams.

timeline.title: Final Day — Hybrid Presentations
timeline.description: The final day was conducted in hybrid mode, with teams participating from across India. Finalists presented their research outcomes and prototypes to a panel of judges from institutional and clinical backgrounds.

timeline.title: Felicitation & Closing
timeline.description: Presentation of awards to the top research teams. Recognition of outstanding problem formulation, research methodology, and deployment potential.

intelligence.headline: What 250 Engineers Told Us.
intelligence.body: Before any team competed, 250+ engineers from institutions across India submitted structured research abstracts — their analysis of real, unresolved clinical problems. The submission pool revealed five dominant clinical problem clusters, each independently confirmed by Bharati Hospital's clinical staff as a genuine operational gap.
intelligence.footnote: The 250+ abstracts from Cohort 1 are not a participation metric. They are a dataset — structured evidence of how India's engineering graduates understand and frame clinical challenges. A structured analysis of this dataset is in preparation.

outcomes.headline: Cohort 1 Outcomes.
outcomes.body: Twenty finalist teams presented research frameworks and AI prototype architectures across five clinical domains. Top teams produced documented solutions for: plantar pressure mapping in diabetic patients, early warning systems for physiological deterioration, opportunistic osteoporosis screening from routine X-rays, pose-based rehabilitation feedback, and ischemic volume estimation from CT imaging.
outcomes.footnote: Full winner documentation is being compiled for the Cohort 1 archive. For access or media inquiries, contact
